name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-52
title: check-template-schema-producer-consistency.py invoked with no args in post-test-green enforcement loop
description:
During Cycle A of the rabbit-feature re-home (PR #142), the TDD subagent observed a warning during the test-green enforcement loop: check-template-schema-producer-consistency.py is invoked with no positional arguments and emits a warning every cycle. The warning does not fail the cycle but creates noise.

Likely root cause: the script expects positional arguments (presumably a feature dir or similar) but the post-test-green enforcement loop calls it bare. Either the invocation site needs to pass the right args, or the script's no-args behavior needs to be defined (e.g. scan all features by default).

Reproduce: run any rabbit-feature-touch cycle to test-green and observe the post-test-green output for a warning mentioning check-template-schema-producer-consistency.

Related: contract Inv 23 in spec.md says 'check-template-schema-producer-consistency.py MUST reference only producers that exist' — but the actual script-invocation defect is the trigger, not producer references.
